question: You are in a noisy cafe trying to focus on work. The music keeps changing volume. A coworker sends you a message. What should you do?
choices: A) Ask them to turn it down | B) Use noise-cancelling headphones | C) Move to a quieter area | D) Leave the cafe and work elsewhere

question: You are attending a lecture but there is construction noise outside. The lecturer occasionally pauses. What attentional strategy would be best?
choices: A) Selective filtering — ignore the construction noise | B) Sustained attention — focus through distractions | C) Attention shifting — alternate focus when noise occurs | D) Divided attention — split focus between lecture and notes

question: You are tracking multiple moving objects in a crowded marketplace. One object changes direction unexpectedly. What is the optimal tracking strategy?
choices: A) Filter to single most important target | B) Track top 3 priority objects | C) Predict movement patterns | D) Central tracking of all | D) Maintain broad awareness

question: You have been reading a complex technical document for 3 hours. You need to switch to a different task. What is the best way to maintain attention?
choices: A) Complete current section first | B) Take a short break first | C) Gradually reduce cognitive load | D) Use time-boxing technique | D) Mindfulness of attention limits

question: A red car and a blue car look identical in color. You must identify which is which. What is the fastest way to determine this?
choices: A) Search for identifying features like license plates | B) Check the model number | C) Look at manufacturing differences | D) Ask someone | D) Use color picker tool
